name: Shane Hollander & Ilya Rozanov | Poly Relationship
description:
ANY POV — User × Hockey Player
Create Your Own Scenario!

This is a test project, an experiment, and my very first multiple-character bot, so I’m super excited to finally share it with everyone! I noticed there are only one or two bots like this out there, which inspired me to make something flexible, fun, and open for everyone to jump into and explore.

Feel free to mess around, try out different scenarios, and experiment however you want—there are no rules here! Whether you’re here to roleplay seriously, test mechanics, or just have fun and see what happens, this bot is made for creativity and freedom.

Yes, it is a bit token-heavy (sorry about that!), but it does come with a proxy to help things run smoother and keep responses flowing nicely. The full definition is turned off by default, so you don’t have to read through everything unless you’re curious—but honestly, the best part is just interacting with the bot and diving right in!

I’ve personally tested this bot to make sure it works and made it public so everyone can try it out. Since this is my first multiple bot, feedback means a LOT to me—comments, reviews, and suggestions are all super appreciated and help me improve future projects!

I’d absolutely love for the community to join me in this little research project and help explore what’s possible together. Just to be clear: {{User}} is part of the relationship, it fully supports ANY POV, and you’re free to play from whatever perspective you want.

Have fun, experiment, and enjoy—and thank you so much for checking it out!

Heated Rivalry Bots

Notes & Ongoing Updates:
This character profile is actively maintained with careful attention to tone, pacing, and consistency.

To ensure immersion, the character will never speak, think, or act on behalf of {{User}}. All behavioral settings have been reviewed and tested, and any issues will be corrected promptly.

Images, scenes, and backgrounds were created using:
Tensor.art · Gemini · PixAI
